summary: Completed full backend and frontend testing of Job Hunt Duo (US-01 to US-06). All 15 backend API tests passed. Frontend loads correctly with all major flows working.

minor.issue: Django Django50Warning: USE_TZ default will change in Django 5.0 - set USE_TZ explicitly in settings.py

ui_bugs.issue: Browser's native file input ('Choose File') is visible alongside the drop zone - not fully styled/hidden. Minor cosmetic issue.

integration_issues.issue: Two 404 errors appear in console on dashboard load (likely for avatar/profile image or other static resources). Not blocking functionality.

issues: Navigation renders as a horizontal top bar rather than a sidebar at 1920px viewport - layout may be collapsing to mobile style. Sidebar labels compress into icons+text on same line.

action_items: Investigate 404 console errors on dashboard (check what resources are failing to load)
Consider hiding native file input or styling it better on CV page
Add USE_TZ = False (or True) explicitly in Django settings to suppress Django 5.0 deprecation warning
Review sidebar layout at wide viewports - may be rendering as horizontal nav instead of sidebar
critical_code_review_comments: Django settings missing explicit USE_TZ setting - will cause behavioral change in Django 5.0
Dashboard appears to render blank content area at times (screenshot showed empty dashboard with only header) - may be a loading state issue where checklist/stats don't re-render after auth
CV upload uses raw %PDF-1.4 header check - ensure robust PDF validation in cv_parser.py

success_rate.frontend: 90% - all pages load and core flows work; minor console 404s and layout note

context_for_next_testing_agent: All US-01 to US-06 backend endpoints tested and passing. Frontend auth, dashboard, CV upload page, preferences page, and logout all working. Remaining issues: 404 console errors on dashboard (low priority), native file input visible on CV page (cosmetic). No mocked APIs.
rca_of_issues: 404 console errors on dashboard are likely from missing static assets or favicon/manifest resources, not related to API calls. The sidebar appearing as horizontal nav is likely because the layout uses flex row at top of screen rather than a fixed sidebar - this appears to be the intended design (horizontal nav bar with icons).
